transcript.text: I’ve found the key runtime edges already: one provider in `main.tsx`, root CSS in `index.css`, a fetching hook, `localStorage`, `IntersectionObserver`, document listeners, clipboard/share, `window.location.reload`, and an imperative download link. I’m reading those concrete implementations now so the default preview can absorb them globally and keep the stories themselves very thin.

The real app path is clearer now: `App` mounts one scrolling page shell, likes state lives in context/localStorage, articles come from Wikipedia via `useWikiArticles`, language choice also lives in localStorage and forces reload, and the biggest browser-specific pieces are the observer, download click, share/clipboard fallback, and document-level dropdown close handler. I’m checking how much of the rest of the tree is actually used so we only write stories for real patterns, not dead/demo code.

transcript.text: The first browser run exposed something useful about the real app shape under Storybook: with the copied `StrictMode` wrapper, `App` fetches more than once on mount, so my fixed mock batch produced duplicate cards. I’m tightening the shared mock to behave more like the live Wikipedia flow by returning different batches per request, then I’ll rerun the same screenshot tests.

The first adjustment didn’t apply cleanly because the mock data file was smaller than the patch expected, so I’m re-reading the exact file contents and applying a narrower patch. After that I’ll rerun the same storybook command to confirm the fetch batches and story assertions are stable.

The mock batcher is now returning different Wikipedia-style pages per request, which should remove the duplicate-key churn while keeping the preview faithful to the app’s random-feed behavior. I’m re-running typecheck and the same Storybook screenshot suite to make sure that fixed both the warnings and the unstable assertions.

content.text: The first browser run exposed something useful about the real app shape under Storybook: with the copied `StrictMode` wrapper, `App` fetches more than once on mount, so my fixed mock batch produced duplicate cards. I’m tightening the shared mock to behave more like the live Wikipedia flow by returning different batches per request, then I’ll rerun the same screenshot tests.

The first adjustment didn’t apply cleanly because the mock data file was smaller than the patch expected, so I’m re-reading the exact file contents and applying a narrower patch. After that I’ll rerun the same storybook command to confirm the fetch batches and story assertions are stable.

File changes:
- add /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-a8efb5db/project/frontend/.storybook/mockWikiData.ts
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-a8efb5db/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx
- update /Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-20-16Z-a8efb5db/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx

The mock batcher is now returning different Wikipedia-style pages per request, which should remove the duplicate-key churn while keeping the preview faithful to the app’s random-feed behavior. I’m re-running typecheck and the same Storybook screenshot suite to make sure that fixed both the warnings and the unstable assertions.
